key_findings: Dual-store write (Neo4j + ClickHouse) has a structural integrity gap at the inter-store boundary: a partial write (Neo4j success, ClickHouse fail) produces cross-store drift detectable only via explicit per-batch reconciliation — this is not guarded today
Neo4j MERGE semantics are idempotent for node creation but NOT if any ON MATCH SET clause uses += or list-append patterns — code audit required before concurrency ramp
ClickHouse INSERT is not transactional; duplicate rows exist between INSERT and background merge; all reconciliation queries must use FINAL keyword or results are incorrect
HTTP result dedup key using full body hash is vulnerable to dynamic content (timestamps, session IDs in HTML) — will produce duplicate HttpResult nodes under real-world scan conditions
Relationship MERGE will implicitly create minimal nodes if either endpoint is missing — endpoint pre-confirmation guard is required before every relationship MERGE
Per-batch reconciliation (not end-of-run) is mandatory — end-of-run detection allows millions of corrupt records to be ingested before discovery
All fqdn inputs must be normalized at Controller boundary (lowercase, strip trailing dot, punycode IDN conversion) before any dedup key computation
Idempotency replay test (Suite 6) must pass with zero new duplicates before first concurrency ramp — this is the pre-ramp gate

decisions.title: Neo4j-first write order as authority contract
decisions.summary: Neo4j write must complete before ClickHouse write; ClickHouse write is skipped on Neo4j failure
decisions.rationale: Neo4j is the graph authority; ClickHouse is telemetry. Graph-without-telemetry is recoverable; telemetry-without-graph corrupts downstream graph queries

decisions.title: ON MATCH SET must use = not +=
decisions.summary: All Neo4j property updates on MATCH must use assignment operator; list-append patterns break idempotency under replay
decisions.rationale: Accumulated values on replay produce incorrect data without any error signal; silent corruption risk

decisions.title: Structural body fingerprint for HTTP dedup key
decisions.summary: HTTP result dedup key uses structural fingerprint of first 512 bytes (stripped of session patterns) instead of full body sha256
decisions.rationale: Full body hash produces different keys for same page on each scan due to dynamic content; structural fingerprint is stable across scans of the same logical page

decisions.title: Endpoint pre-confirmation before relationship MERGE
decisions.summary: Controller must verify source and target nodes exist in Neo4j before issuing any relationship MERGE
decisions.rationale: Neo4j MERGE creates implicit nodes if endpoints are missing; implicit nodes lack required properties and corrupt graph topology

decisions.title: Per-batch reconciliation with 30s ClickHouse settle delay
decisions.summary: Validate lane fires after every ingest batch; 30s settle before ClickHouse FINAL query to allow ReplacingMergeTree merge
decisions.rationale: End-of-run detection allows massive corruption accumulation; per-batch detection bounds blast radius to one batch (≤5,000 items)

decisions.title: Quarantine on filesystem, not database
decisions.summary: Failed integrity items written to artifacts/quarantine/ filesystem path with full forensic context
decisions.rationale: DB-based quarantine fails when DB is unavailable; filesystem quarantine works during datastore failure — exactly when quarantine is most needed

decisions.title: TTL bucketing for DNS record dedup key
decisions.summary: DNS record dedup key uses floor(ttl/300)*300 TTL bucket, not exact TTL value
decisions.rationale: Exact TTL produces different dedup keys as TTL decrements across scans; 5-minute bucketing collapses the key space to stable windows

decisions.title: ingested_batch_id added to both stores
decisions.summary: Neo4j node properties and ClickHouse rows both carry ingested_batch_id field for per-batch reconciliation
decisions.rationale: Without batch ID field, cross-store reconciliation requires full-table scans; batch ID enables targeted O(batch_size) reconciliation

dedup_key_contracts.clickhouse_row: ReplacingMergeTree ORDER BY (fqdn, scan_type, toStartOfHour(scan_ts)) version=unix_ts_ingest

idempotency_contracts.neo4j_node_update: MERGE ON MATCH SET n.prop = $val — idempotent; FORBIDDEN: ON MATCH SET n.list += $val
idempotency_contracts.neo4j_relationship: MERGE (a)-[r:TYPE]->(b) — idempotent IF both endpoints exist; FORBIDDEN: relationship MERGE without endpoint pre-confirmation
idempotency_contracts.neo4j_transaction: Atomic batch of ≤500 ops; full rollback on any error; safe to retry
idempotency_contracts.clickhouse_insert: NOT idempotent by default; duplicates persist until background merge; use checkpoint store to prevent double-insert; FINAL in all reconciliation queries

clickhouse.dedup_handling: ReplacingMergeTree background merge; FINAL in queries

inter_store.on_crash_between_neo4j_and_clickhouse: checkpoint status=PENDING detected; resume from checkpoint; insert ClickHouse only
inter_store.on_neo4j_failure: skip ClickHouse write; route batch to ingest-DLQ

suite_1_duplicate_detection.neo4j_subdomain_dupe: MATCH (n:Subdomain) WITH n.name AS name, count(n) AS cnt WHERE cnt > 1 RETURN name, cnt ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT 100
suite_1_duplicate_detection.neo4j_dns_dupe: MATCH (n:Subdomain)-[:HAS_DNS]->(d:DnsRecord) WITH n.name AS fqdn, d.record_type AS rtype, d.value AS rval, count(d) AS cnt WHERE cnt > 1 RETURN fqdn, rtype, rval, cnt ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT 100
suite_1_duplicate_detection.clickhouse_dupe: SELECT fqdn, scan_type, toStartOfHour(scan_ts) AS hour, count(*) AS cnt FROM subdomains WHERE scan_ts >= now() - INTERVAL 1 HOUR GROUP BY fqdn, scan_type, hour HAVING cnt > 1 ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT 100

suite_2_cross_store_reconciliation.neo4j_query: MATCH (n:Subdomain) WHERE n.ingested_batch_id = $batch_id RETURN count(n) AS neo4j_count
suite_2_cross_store_reconciliation.clickhouse_query: SELECT count(*) AS ch_count FROM subdomains FINAL WHERE ingested_batch_id = %(batch_id)s

suite_3_orphan_detection.neo4j_orphan_subdomains: MATCH (n:Subdomain) WHERE NOT (n)-[:HAS_DNS]->() AND n.created_at < datetime() - duration('PT1H') RETURN count(n) AS orphan_count
suite_3_orphan_detection.neo4j_orphan_dns_records: MATCH (d:DnsRecord) WHERE NOT ()-[:HAS_DNS]->(d) RETURN count(d) AS orphan_dns_count

suite_4_partial_write_detection.ch_query: SELECT DISTINCT fqdn FROM subdomains FINAL WHERE scan_ts >= now() - INTERVAL 1 HOUR ORDER BY rand() LIMIT 1000

suite_5_checkpoint_integrity.stale_pending_query: SELECT batch_id, lane, status, ts FROM checkpoints WHERE status = 'PENDING' AND ts < datetime('now', '-5 minutes') ORDER BY ts ASC
suite_5_checkpoint_integrity.incomplete_query: SELECT count(*) AS incomplete FROM checkpoints WHERE status != 'COMPLETE' AND status != 'QUARANTINED' AND ts < datetime('now', '-15 minutes')

suite_7_rollback_verification.clickhouse_query: SELECT count(*) FROM subdomains FINAL WHERE ingested_batch_id = %(failed_batch_id)s
suite_7_rollback_verification.expected: zero (transaction rolled back, ClickHouse write skipped)

risk_model.title: Accumulating property SET breaks idempotency
risk_model.mitigation: Code audit: find all ON MATCH SET += patterns; replace with = before concurrency ramp

risk_model.title: Dynamic body content creates duplicate HTTP nodes
risk_model.mitigation: Structural body fingerprint (first 512B stripped) instead of full body hash

risk_model.title: Relationship MERGE creates implicit nodes
risk_model.mitigation: Endpoint pre-confirmation guard before every relationship MERGE

risk_model.title: Cross-store drift undetected between batches
risk_model.mitigation: Per-batch reconciliation in validate lane; not end-of-run

risk_model.title: ClickHouse FINAL cost spike under heavy load
risk_model.mitigation: Schedule full-table FINAL queries off-peak; per-batch FINAL on small batch windows is acceptable

risk_model.title: Stale PENDING checkpoints from crashed sessions
risk_model.mitigation: ORS monitor: PENDING > 5 min = CRITICAL; resume procedure defined

risk_model.title: ClickHouse orphan rows from write-order violation
risk_model.mitigation: Write order enforced as code contract; Suite 4 detects post-facto

risk_model.title: fqdn normalization inconsistency across code paths
risk_model.mitigation: Single normalization function at Controller ingest boundary; never normalize at DB layer

recommended_actions.action: Code audit: replace all ON MATCH SET += with = in Neo4j write code before concurrency ramp
Replace HTTP result body fingerprint with structural fingerprint of first 512B stripped of session patterns
Add endpoint pre-confirmation guard before every Neo4j relationship MERGE
Add ingested_batch_id to both Neo4j node properties and ClickHouse rows during write path
Implement Suite 2 reconciliation in validate lane with FINAL keyword and 30s settle delay
Implement all 7 validation suites; integrate Suite 1, 5 as continuous ORS monitors
Run Suite 6 idempotency replay test; must pass with zero duplicates before concurrency ramp
Add ORS monitor for stale PENDING checkpoints: WARNING 3 min, CRITICAL 5 min
Enforce single fqdn normalization function at Controller ingest boundary (lowercase, strip dot, punycode)
Add ORS DLQ depth monitor per lane: WARNING 500, CRITICAL 2000 items

assumptions: A1: Controller has write access to both Neo4j and ClickHouse from a single process
A3: ClickHouse table uses ReplacingMergeTree with version column (monotonically increasing Unix timestamp)
A4: ingested_batch_id field can be added to Neo4j node properties and ClickHouse rows without breaking existing schema
A6: Checkpoint store accessible from Controller at all times; failure halts ingest
A7: fqdn normalization at Controller boundary does not break existing data (existing data can be re-normalized without re-keying)
